question.text: SEBI mandates that listed companies must declare dividends in:

answer.text: Rupees per share on a per-share basis

explanation.text: **The Logic:** SEBI has mandated listed companies shall declare dividends in rupees per share, not as a percentage of face value, to avoid confusion among investors. **The Trap:** Previously dividends were declared as percentages. SEBI changed this to per-share amounts for clarity. **Cross-Exam Context:** Dividend declaration rules tested in NISM XV Ch 9. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** A 50% dividend on Rs.2 face value = Re.1/share. Same 50% on Rs.10 face value = Rs.5/share. Per-share declaration eliminates this confusion.
